evals.prompt: What are the main negative events for financial companies that reported in the past 2 weeks?
evals.expected_output: A plan that: (1) uses getAnalytics with sectors: ['Financials'], sentiment: ['negative'], and a 2-week date range to get top negative event types, (2) calls searchTopCompanies once per event type to find companies with the most occurrences of each event, (3) uses search to retrieve supporting quotes per company, (4) includes citation links in format [N][N] pointing to https://dev.prontonlp.com/#/ref/<ID>.

assertions.name: uses_get_analytics_first
assertions.description: getAnalytics is called with sectors: ['Financials'] and sentiment: ['negative'] to identify event types

assertions.name: search_top_companies_per_event
assertions.description: searchTopCompanies is called once per event type (not once for all events combined)

assertions.name: citations_present
assertions.description: Response includes reference-style markdown links [N][N] pointing to https://dev.prontonlp.com/#/ref/<ID>

assertions.name: at_least_5_examples
assertions.description: Response includes at least 5 companies or events as examples with supporting quotes

assertions.name: no_tool_names_in_response
assertions.description: Tool names (getAnalytics, searchTopCompanies, etc.) do not appear in the user-facing response

evals.prompt: What companies might benefit from generative AI?
evals.expected_output: A plan that: (1) uses search with searchQuery: 'generative AI' and synonyms: ['Gen AI', 'Artificial Intelligence'] and sentiment: 'positive', (2) if fewer than 30 results, re-runs with deepSearch: true, (3) uses addContext on the most relevant result IDs, (4) returns at least 5 companies as examples with supporting quotes and citation links.

assertions.name: search_used_as_fallback
assertions.description: search tool is used since no more specific tool fits a broad AI beneficiary question

assertions.name: deep_search_on_sparse_results
assertions.description: If fewer than 30 results are returned, search is re-run with deepSearch: true

assertions.name: add_context_called
assertions.description: addContext is called on relevant result IDs to get richer content

assertions.name: citations_present
assertions.description: Response includes [N][N] reference-style links pointing to https://dev.prontonlp.com/#/ref/<ID>

assertions.name: at_least_5_company_examples
assertions.description: At least 5 companies are named as examples with supporting quotes or sentences

evals.prompt: What are the top movers this week? Which stocks are worth watching?
evals.expected_output: A plan that: (1) calls getTopMovers to get companies with investment score, sentiment change, and stock price change, (2) calls getCompanyDescription for the top 10-15 most relevant companies from results, (3) optionally uses search for recent positive/negative info, (4) presents companies with empty markdown links containing the company id: 'Company Name [](id)', (5) avoids the words 'hot', 'underperforming', 'overperforming' — uses 'worth monitoring', 'potential buying opportunities', 'potential sell signals' instead.

assertions.name: get_top_movers_called
assertions.description: getTopMovers is the primary tool called

assertions.name: get_company_description_for_top_companies
assertions.description: getCompanyDescription is called for the top 10-15 companies returned by getTopMovers

assertions.name: company_id_links_present
assertions.description: Companies are presented with empty markdown links containing the company id: 'Name [](id)'

assertions.name: avoids_banned_words
assertions.description: Response does not use the words 'hot', 'underperforming', or 'overperforming'

assertions.name: uses_approved_language
assertions.description: Response uses 'worth monitoring', 'potential buying opportunities', or 'potential sell signals'

evals.prompt: Who were the most positive speakers in Apple's last earnings call? And which companies did they talk about most?
evals.expected_output: A plan that: (1) calls getSpeakers for Apple with speakerTypes: ['Analysts'], sortBy: 'sentiment', sortOrder: 'desc', (2) extracts speakerIds from results, (3) calls searchTopCompanies ONCE PER speakerId (never batched in one call), (4) surfaces top companies per speaker individually.

assertions.name: get_speakers_called
assertions.description: getSpeakers is called for Apple with Analysts speaker type sorted by sentiment

assertions.name: search_top_companies_per_speaker_id
assertions.description: searchTopCompanies is called once per speakerId — never passing multiple speakerIds in one call

assertions.name: speaker_ids_extracted_and_passed
assertions.description: speakerIds from getSpeakers results are correctly extracted and passed to searchTopCompanies
